mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month payroll employment growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market expansion and consumer income growth, which drives aggregate demand and corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: post-recession recovery with labor market stabilization
failure_condition: Payroll growth exceeds 150k persons month-over-month in > 70% of observations yet 20-day forward returns are negative or not significantly different from zero (t-test p > 0.05) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate held at or near zero percent signals an extended monetary accommodation regime that suppresses risk-free rates and incentivizes equity risk-taking, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as portfolio rebalancing into equities accelerates.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25% for 6 consecutive months
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary accommodation post-financial crisis
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25% yet 60-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of triggered periods, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) combined with accelerating housing starts (HOUST) indicates strengthening consumer and residential demand, signaling cyclical expansion that lifts corporate profit expectations and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5% AND HOUST month-over-month growth > 5%
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 407,805M and HOUST at 699k units as of January 2012
regime_hint: early-cycle demand recovery with housing stabilization
failure_condition: Both RSAFS and HOUST growth conditions are met simultaneously in > 60% of months yet 20-day forward returns are negative or show Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declining month-over-month signals supply-side weakness and potential demand destruction, which compresses profit margins and forward earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as growth concerns emerge.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines month-over-month by more than 0.3% in > 65% of observations yet 5-day forward returns are positive or show Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 8.5% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer confidence, which dampens discretionary spending and corporate revenue growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as recession risk premia increase.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.4 percentage points in 2 consecutive months
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recessionary pressure
failure_condition: UNRATE rises > 0.4pp in 2 consecutive months in > 55% of triggered instances yet 20-day forward returns are positive or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI (CPIAUCSL) accelerating above 2.5% year-over-year combined with the federal funds rate held below 0.5% creates negative real rates that erode fixed-income returns and force equity risk premium compression, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as real asset demand increases.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 227.505 and FEDFUNDS at 0.1% as of January-February 2012
regime_hint: negative real rate regime with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: Both conditions (CPI YoY > 2.5% and FEDFUNDS < 0.5%) are met in > 50% of months yet 60-day forward returns are negative or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly at or above 2.5% annualized rate signals sustained economic expansion and rising corporate revenue potential, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings forecasts are revised upward.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13,429.9B (2017 dollars) as of Q4 2011
regime_hint: sustained economic expansion above trend growth
failure_condition: GDPC1 annualized growth exceeds 2.5% in > 60% of quarters yet 60-day forward returns are negative or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) accelerating month-over-month above 0.4% combined with rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals broad-based supply expansion and labor productivity gains, which support margin expansion and positive earnings surprises, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth > 0.4% AND PAYEMS month-over-month growth > 100k persons
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 95.9054 and PAYEMS at 132,697k as of January 2012
regime_hint: synchronized supply and labor expansion in recovery phase
failure_condition: Both conditions (INDPRO MoM > 0.4% and PAYEMS MoM > 100k) are met in > 65% of months yet 20-day forward returns are negative or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
